Defines the MetricGroup structure and functions to create, destroy, and manage metric groups.

# Purpose
The code defines a C header file that provides an interface for managing a collection of metrics, encapsulated in a `MetricGroup` structure. The `MetricGroup` structure contains lists for names, values, and icons, as well as metadata about the number of items and constraints on the size of these items. This structure is designed to store and manage a set of related metrics, where each metric has a name, a value, and an associated icon.

The file declares three main functions to operate on `MetricGroup` objects. The [`metric_group_create`](<#metric_group_create>) function initializes a new `MetricGroup` with specified limits on the number of items and the maximum string size for names and values. The [`metric_group_destroy`](<#metric_group_destroy>) function deallocates the resources associated with a `MetricGroup`. The [`metric_group_add_item`](<#metric_group_add_item>) function adds a new metric to the group, using internationalization (i18n) keys for the name and a specified icon. This function returns a boolean indicating whether the addition was successful. The header file includes dependencies on `attribute.h` and `timeline_resources.h`, suggesting that it relies on types or functions defined in these files.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `attribute.h`
- `timeline_resources.h`


# Data Structures

---
### MetricGroup
- **Type**: ``struct``
- **Members**:
    - ``names``: Pointer to a `StringList` that holds the names of the metrics.
    - ``values``: Pointer to a `StringList` that holds the values of the metrics.
    - ``icons``: Pointer to a `Uint32List` that holds the icons associated with the metrics.
    - ``num_items``: Integer that represents the current number of items in the metric group.
    - ``max_num_items``: Integer that specifies the maximum number of items that can be added to the metric group.
    - ``max_item_string_size``: Size_t that defines the maximum length of any string in the group, including names and values.
- **Description**: Represents a group of metrics, each with a name, value, and icon. The `MetricGroup` structure manages a collection of metrics, allowing for the addition of new items up to a specified maximum. It uses `StringList` for storing names and values, and `Uint32List` for storing icons, with constraints on the number of items and the size of strings.


# Function Declarations (Public API)

---
### metric\_group\_create<!-- {{#callable_declaration:metric_group_create}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/services/normal/timeline/metricgroup.h#L35>)

Creates a new metric group with specified capacity and string size limits.
- **Description**: Use this function to allocate and initialize a new `MetricGroup` structure with specified limits on the number of items and the maximum string size for each item. This function must be called before using the metric group in any operations. Ensure that `max_num_items` and `max_item_string_size` are set according to the expected usage to avoid allocation failures. The caller is responsible for managing the memory of the returned `MetricGroup` and must call `metric_group_destroy` to free resources when the metric group is no longer needed.
- **Inputs**:
    - `max_num_items`: Specifies the maximum number of items that the metric group can hold. Must be a positive integer. If the value is invalid, the function may fail to allocate memory.
    - `max_item_string_size`: Specifies the maximum length of any string (name or value) in the group. Must be a positive size_t value. If the value is invalid, the function may fail to allocate memory.
- **Output**: Returns a pointer to a newly allocated `MetricGroup` structure. The caller is responsible for freeing this memory.
- **See Also**: [`metric_group_create`](<metricgroup.c.md#metric_group_create>)  (Implementation)


---
### metric\_group\_destroy<!-- {{#callable_declaration:metric_group_destroy}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/services/normal/timeline/metricgroup.h#L39>)

Releases resources associated with a metric group.
- **Description**: Use this function to release all resources associated with a `MetricGroup` when it is no longer needed. This function must be called to prevent memory leaks. It safely handles a null pointer by performing no action, making it safe to call even if the `MetricGroup` has already been destroyed or was never allocated.
- **Inputs**:
    - `metric_group`: Pointer to the `MetricGroup` to destroy. Can be null, in which case the function does nothing. The caller must ensure that the `MetricGroup` is not used after this function is called.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`metric_group_destroy`](<metricgroup.c.md#metric_group_destroy>)  (Implementation)


---
### metric\_group\_add\_item<!-- {{#callable_declaration:metric_group_add_item}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/services/normal/timeline/metricgroup.h#L48>)

Adds an item to a metric group.
- **Description**: Use this function to add a new item to a `MetricGroup`. The function requires that the `MetricGroup` has been created and not yet reached its maximum number of items. It adds the name, value, and icon to the respective lists within the `MetricGroup`. If the `MetricGroup` is full, the function will not add the item and will return `false`. Ensure that the `name_i18n` is a valid internationalization key and that `i18n_owner` is correctly set to retrieve the name string.
- **Inputs**:
    - `metric_group`: Pointer to the `MetricGroup` to which the item will be added. Must not be null and must be initialized.
    - `name_i18n`: Internationalization key for the name string. Must be a valid key that can be resolved by the `i18n_owner`.
    - `value`: String representing the value field. Must not exceed the maximum item string size defined in the `MetricGroup`.
    - `icon`: `TimelineResourceId` representing the icon ID to associate with the item.
    - `i18n_owner`: Pointer to the internationalization owner used to resolve the `name_i18n` key. Must be valid and correctly configured.
- **Output**: Returns `true` if the item was successfully added; returns `false` if the `MetricGroup` is full.
- **See Also**: [`metric_group_add_item`](<metricgroup.c.md#metric_group_add_item>)  (Implementation)
